summary: Define bounded M6 Alpha system-complete contract
acceptance_criteria: Define the minimum M6 Alpha contract for a start-to-victory playthrough, including start conditions, one accepted victory condition, failure conditions, evidence requirements, and no-placeholder major system expectations.
Map the contract to accepted M1-M5 systems and the M6 DAG without adding product code, content-source data, dependencies, release artifacts, or roadmap changes.

Preserve DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE exactly: manual node battle may appear only as a post-1.0 non-blocking candidate note and not as M6/M7/M8 implementation, spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency.

Preserve Human Gates for frozen product, platform, core architecture, paid services, accounts or secrets, server or multiplayer, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, commercial or branding decisions, major cultural-risk decisions, irreversible migrations, and any formal 1.0 scope reversal.
required_tests: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M6-ALPHA-SYSTEM-CONTRACT-001

target.name: M6 Alpha system-complete contract v0
target.objective: Define the minimum Alpha proof that Monsoon Sovereigns can start a bounded scenario, reach one accepted victory condition, explain failure, and demonstrate all major M6 systems without placeholder surfaces or hidden rule shortcuts.
target.alpha_minimum: One complete start-to-victory path is required; the path may be narrow, but every major system in the M6 DAG must either participate in the path or expose a non-placeholder reason-coded state that can affect it.
target.non_painting_rule: Changing actual controller or coloring a district is never sufficient. Victory requires recognized political settlement, legitimacy or diplomatic acceptance evidence, obligation or governance terms, and a stabilization check.
target.planning_only_policy: This task defines contract only. It adds no product code, package code, content-source data, dependencies, roadmap edits, accepted ADR edits, gate edits, release artifacts, telemetry, server behavior, or arbitrary-code mod support.
target.historical_policy: This contract introduces no formal historical facts, names, symbols, claims, language, art, music, or culture-specific assets. Any later formal scenario detail must route to historical_researcher and use HISTORICAL, INFERRED, COMPOSITE, or FICTIONAL labels.
target.manual_node_battle_policy: DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE is preserved exactly. Manual node battle may appear only as a post-1.0 non-blocking candidate note and is not an M6, M7, or M8 implementation, spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency.

scenario_set.content_policy: Scenario records may be COMPOSITE or FICTIONAL for Alpha. Formal historical claims require historical_researcher review before acceptance.

authoritative_basis: M1 deterministic WorldState, GameDay scheduler, Command/Event/Query v1, RNG, save envelope, replay and state hash.
M2 economy, population, labor, agriculture, grain/cash source ledgers, routes, transport and seasonal pressure.
M3 polities, suzerain chains, offices, characters, obligations, administrative burden, succession pressure, and postwar governance.
M4 campaign objectives, faction knowledge, muster, grain supply, route capacity, march/supply state, automatic engagement, siege choice, withdrawal, war outcome, postwar candidate and AI trace.
M5 playable loop, client command/preview surfaces, Web/Windows smoke evidence, performance evidence, local player research limits, and PASS_WITH_LIMITS carry-forward notes.
required_initial_state: A deterministic seed, start day, scenario id, fixture or manifest hash, and start state hash are recorded before the first player command.

The player court has at least one vassal or dependent polity, at least one contested target, at least one eligible office or agent decision, visible population/labor/grain/cash constraints, and one plausible campaign or diplomatic route.
At least one legitimacy or diplomatic recognition issue, one succession or continuity pressure, one policy/event choice, and one encyclopedia reference are reachable before victory or failure.
At least one rival or non-player polity can act through the same command surface or choose a reason-coded wait/no-action path.
No active Human Gate is required to begin the Alpha playthrough; if a Human Gate scope is encountered, the run records BLOCKED rather than treating it as success.

scale_starting_assumption.starting_hypothesis: Three Alpha scenarios, each with enough districts, polities, characters, offices, obligations, route edges, and policy/event definitions to exercise the major systems, but smaller than final M7 content lock.
scale_starting_assumption.metric: A complete run exposes all major M6 system states without forcing more than one winning path or hiding missing systems behind placeholder text.
scale_starting_assumption.validation_way: M6 scenario pipeline validation, start-to-victory replay, client Alpha surface smoke, and Alpha no-P0/P1 validation evidence.

accepted_victory_condition.statement: The player wins when a contested target or dependent polity is brought into a stable recognized order through diplomacy, legitimacy, campaign outcome, succession continuity, and postwar or vassal governance terms.
minimum_requirements: A valid political arrangement exists: vassal settlement, recognized succession-backed settlement, restored ruler settlement, tribute/obligation settlement, or direct control with explicit administrative and garrison burden.

The arrangement survives the stabilization window without unresolved suzerain cycle, duplicate same-district settlement, impossible obligation ledger, broken succession continuity, or immediate legitimacy collapse.

The player has made at least one meaningful decision in agent appointment, diplomacy or obligation, population/labor or supply, monsoon campaign planning, succession or legitimacy, policy/event handling, and postwar or vassal settlement.
AI or adviser reasons can explain at least one rival action, wait, refusal, withdrawal, policy choice, or no-action decision that influenced the path.
Replay evidence includes start hash, ordered command sequence, save/load checkpoint, terminal state, final hash, and matching Node/Worker-compatible deterministic result.

stabilization_window_starting_assumption.starting_hypothesis: Six to twelve in-game months after the settlement, long enough for at least one obligation, legitimacy, administrative, or policy consequence to surface.
stabilization_window_starting_assumption.metric: The victory state is not immediately voided by arrears, succession break, invalid settlement, supply ledger violation, or legitimacy collapse.
stabilization_window_starting_assumption.validation_way: M6 start-to-victory sim loop tests, batch auto-run result review, and Alpha no-P0/P1 validation.

failure_conditions: The player loses all maintainable power centers or has no valid succession, regency, stewardship, or political continuity path.
The contested target changes actual control but lacks a valid recognized arrangement, obligation term, legitimacy basis, or stabilization evidence.
Campaign, siege, withdrawal, season, supply, or population/labor consequences collapse the playable objective and no diplomatic or political recovery remains inside the scenario contract.
Diplomacy or legitimacy falls into an unrecoverable breach state caused by explicit decisions, not a hidden global punishment.
Succession produces no eligible continuity path, creates cyclic or duplicated claims, or invalidates command authority before victory is locked.
A policy/event choice creates an explicit terminal failure or removes every legal route to recognized settlement.
Replay, save/load, Node/Worker hash parity, command parity, source-ledger conservation, or a listed invariant fails.

A required Human Gate is encountered without approval, including manual node battle reversal, telemetry, accounts, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, paid services, branding, commercial release, frozen architecture/platform/core changes, irreversible migration, major cultural-risk decision, or formal 1.0 scope reversal.

player_decisions.decision: Choose one of the Alpha scenarios and decide whether to pursue recognition through diplomacy-first, campaign-first, or recovery-first sequencing.
player_decisions.advantage: The opening choice lets the player commit to a readable strategic problem instead of a single scripted tutorial route.
player_decisions.cost: Each opening exposes different political, succession, route, and resource liabilities that cannot all be solved before the first crisis.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires validated scenario metadata, start hash, goal text, known starting pressures, and non-placeholder read models for diplomacy, legitimacy, succession, policy/event, and campaign state.
player_decisions.counter: The rival can act on the same opening pressures; a weak opening can be punished by wait, refusal, succession contest, or supply timing rather than by hidden difficulty bonuses.
player_decisions.design_guard: Openings are not cultural or ethnic buffs. Differences come from explicit institutions, geography, obligations, resources, relationships, and scenario labels.

player_decisions.decision: Appoint, retain, or reassign an agent for office, district, campaign, diplomacy, or succession support.
player_decisions.advantage: A fitting agent improves a specific decision path such as negotiation reliability, office execution, local compliance, campaign preparation, or succession support.
player_decisions.cost: Appointment consumes political capacity, may displace another holder, creates relationship obligations, can empower a local bloc, and can worsen administrative burden.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires eligible office or mandate, candidate reason codes, relationship and interest context, policy continuity, jurisdiction, and preview of institutional consequences.
player_decisions.counter: A rival claimant, local elite, overloaded court, or offended office holder can reduce compliance, contest legitimacy, or refuse later obligations.
player_decisions.design_guard: Characters are not total attribute cards. Public ability values can inform fit, but interests, relationships, office context, legitimacy, information quality, and institutional limits must remain visible.

player_decisions.decision: Offer recognition, protection, alliance-like terms if represented by accepted schemas, guarantee terms if represented by accepted schemas, tribute terms, access, grain, troops, or lighter obligations through accepted command paths.
player_decisions.advantage: Diplomacy can convert influence into support or neutrality without direct annexation and can make victory possible without painting the map.
player_decisions.cost: Terms create explicit future obligations, legitimacy exposure, arrears risk, hostage or guarantee risk if later implemented, and opportunity cost against other dependents.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires M6 diplomacy/legitimacy state, M3 suzerain and obligation accounting, visible relationship/claim reasons, no graph cycles, and serializable acceptance/refusal reasons.
player_decisions.counter: The target or rival may refuse, delay, demand better terms, exploit weak legitimacy, or accept terms that become costly when succession or supply conditions change.
player_decisions.design_guard: No hidden global reputation penalty. Diplomatic consequences must attach to specific terms, actors, obligations, claims, relationships, and events.

player_decisions.decision: Support a claimant, arrange temporary stewardship, resolve disputed succession, or defer a settlement until legitimacy evidence improves.
player_decisions.advantage: A timely legitimacy or succession settlement can preserve command authority, vassal recognition, office continuity, and postwar settlement validity.
player_decisions.cost: Backing one path can alienate another faction, weaken a dependent, delay campaign timing, or create future claim disputes.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires deterministic succession candidate generation, explicit eligibility and claim reasons, diplomacy/legitimacy effects, relationship support sources, and save/replay coverage.
player_decisions.counter: Rivals can back an alternate claimant, dependents can refuse to inherit old obligations, and delayed settlement can shrink the campaign or harvest window.
player_decisions.design_guard: Succession is a network and institution problem, not a single global stability score.

player_decisions.decision: Commit households, labor, retainers, grain, cash, storage, and transport capacity to support diplomacy, campaign, siege, or stabilization.
player_decisions.advantage: Proper mobilization increases operating days, siege endurance, local compliance, and recovery options after a failed route.
player_decisions.cost: Mobilized households reduce productive labor, grain reservations create spoilage or opportunity risk, retainers create recurring fiscal pressure, and transport commitments can starve other obligations.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires M2 population/economy and M4 supply source ledgers, seasonal calendar, route capacity, muster state, and explicit reserve/consume/release events.
player_decisions.counter: An opponent can wait out supplies, force a late withdrawal, target overextended obligations, or exploit weakened harvest and local labor recovery.
player_decisions.design_guard: No abstract manpower pool or hidden global war exhaustion. Costs must be visible in local population, labor, harvest, grain, cash, obligation, casualty, or transport ledgers.

player_decisions.decision: Pick objective, route, transport mode, staging site, campaign start day, siege approach, or cancellation before sunk costs dominate.
player_decisions.advantage: A well-matched route and window can beat monsoon risk, enemy reinforcement, grain exhaustion, or siege delay.
player_decisions.cost: Plans tie up muster and grain, can become stale, can expose intent, and may require cancellation with recorded opportunity costs.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires M4 campaign objective and faction knowledge, M2/M4 route and transport forecasts, calendar/season state, supply preview, and reason-coded cancellation/withdrawal outcomes.
player_decisions.counter: Weather window, river or road capacity, stale knowledge, enemy relief, vassal delay, or supply shortage can make waiting, changing target, or withdrawing correct.
player_decisions.design_guard: Monsoon is a route, timing, disease/siege/supply, and transport planning force, not a universal percentage debuff.

player_decisions.decision: Commit to march, accept automatic engagement, select siege pressure, negotiate, hold, assault-like abstract choice if already accepted by M4 rules, or withdraw.
player_decisions.advantage: The war loop can create a postwar candidate or diplomatic leverage without direct permanent annexation.
player_decisions.cost: Casualties, grain loss, morale or credit hooks, siege damage, breach consequences, and withdrawal losses flow back into institutions and local ledgers.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires accepted M4 automatic engagement and siege rules, campaign/march/supply state, deterministic tie-breakers, source-ledger conservation, and visible war outcome handoff.
player_decisions.counter: The defender can use fortification, time, relief, monsoon pressure, or the attacker's political need to withdraw.
player_decisions.design_guard: Manual node battle remains unavailable. The Alpha contract uses deterministic automatic resolution and strategic siege choices only.

player_decisions.decision: Choose direct control, restored dependent ruler, tribute/obligation settlement, policy concession, event option, or delayed settlement after a war or diplomatic result.
player_decisions.advantage: Postwar choice converts temporary leverage into different mixes of recognition, obligation, administrative reach, and local continuity.
player_decisions.cost: Direct control needs administrative and garrison capacity; restored rulers preserve autonomy and breach risk; tribute-only settlements offer lower control; policy concessions may reduce short-term friction but create future obligations.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires M3 postwar candidate and governance paths, M6 legitimacy/diplomacy state, policy/event runtime, obligation compatibility, and stabilization preview.
player_decisions.counter: Administrative overload, local elite refusal, arrears, succession dispute, rival protection offer, or event consequences can undermine the arrangement.
player_decisions.design_guard: Victory is a political arrangement with terms and failure modes, not a permanent color fill.

authoritative_state: M1 deterministic world, calendar, command/event/query protocol, RNG, save envelope, replay tail, and hash state.
M2 district, economy, population, labor, grain, cash, harvest, storage, route, transport, and seasonal state.
M3 polity, control, suzerain, obligation, office, character, relationship, administrative burden, succession, and postwar governance state.
M4 campaign objective, knowledge, muster, supply, route capacity, march, engagement, siege, withdrawal, war outcome, postwar candidate, and AI trace state.

M6 diplomacy, legitimacy, completed succession resolution, policy/event runtime, encyclopedia reference, scenario manifest, map candidate reference, terminal victory/failure state, batch evidence metadata, and adviser intervention evidence metadata.
state.client_state_rule: Client/UI may hold view state, selection, filters, local drafts, generated local evidence artifact views, and read-model caches only. They must not own or mutate authoritative WorldState.
state.save_rule: Alpha save/load uses explicit accepted save envelope and additive versioned schemas. No irreversible migration is authorized by this contract.
state.content_state_rule: Scenario and map candidate data enter the simulation only through validated content/runtime boundaries; sim-core does not import Node IO, DOM, React, Pixi, Electron, or real-time APIs.

daily: GameDay advance, march movement, supply consumption, siege pressure, route and seasonal risk, policy/event timers when scheduled, AI decision opportunities, and state hash update.
monthly_or_periodic: Obligation due/arrears review, administrative burden projection, legitimacy drift from explicit institutional causes, succession pressure review, population/labor recovery, harvest/resource cycles, and diplomacy offer expiry.
event_driven: Scenario start, appointment, office policy update, diplomacy offer/response, obligation create/fulfill/breach, succession resolution, policy/event option, encyclopedia reference unlock, campaign objective create/update/cancel, muster response, grain reserve/release/consume, march start, field engagement, siege choice, withdrawal, postwar settlement, victory/failure terminal check, save/load.
per_run_or_batch: Start hash capture, terminal hash capture, no-rescue AI run summary, batch metric aggregation, dashboard artifact generation, adviser intervention evidence recording, Alpha no-P0/P1 classification.
ui_refresh: UI refreshes from read models and reason-code projections after commands, scheduled day advancement, save/load, and local artifact selection; it does not duplicate simulation formula authority.

formula_intent: Every advantage is local, conditional, and inspectable; it must name its cost, dependency, and counterplay.

Do not hide global penalties behind vague stability, reputation, culture, ethnicity, war exhaustion, or legitimacy meters. Consequences must route through institutions: offices, obligations, claims, succession, relationships, ledgers, route capacity, supply, policy/event effects, or postwar terms.
Legitimacy should explain why actors accept, refuse, delay, or contest authority; it is not a generic universal score that silently modifies every formula.
Diplomacy should create explicit terms, promises, recognition, access, obligations, and refusal reasons; it is not a full peace-treaty system in M6.
Policy and events should expose cause, option, consequence, timing, and encyclopedia reference through schemas and reason codes; no script callbacks, hidden managers, or object-graph saves.
AI evaluation should use bounded visible candidates, deterministic tie-breakers, and protocol-visible traces, not hidden resources, hidden map knowledge, or privileged actions.
Characters are institutional actors with interests, relationships, offices, status, local bases, knowledge and constraints; they are not total attribute cards.
Culture, ethnicity, religion, language, or modern identity categories must never be fixed buffs or penalties. Any formal cultural detail requires historical_researcher routing.
invariants: Same scenario id, content manifest hash, seed, start state, and command sequence produce the same final state hash in Node runner and Worker/browser-compatible execution.
All player and AI state changes use the same versioned GameCommand or accepted deterministic scheduler path.
No simulation result depends on Math.random, Date.now, real time, object traversal order, unstable sort order, DOM, React, Pixi, Electron, Node filesystem, or hidden external service state.

One district has one actual controller at a time; suzerain chains, claim references, and diplomacy relation graphs cannot create cycles unless an explicit accepted model allows and validates that relation type.
Obligations, grain, cash, labor, population, troops, casualties, and supply movements conserve explicit source ledgers and reject double counting.
Legitimacy, recognition, and diplomacy effects have reason-coded sources and cannot silently overwrite postwar governance, succession, or obligation state.
Succession candidate ordering is deterministic and invalid candidates are rejected with serializable reasons.
Policy/event effects are schema-defined, deterministic, bounded, serializable, and cannot inject arbitrary code or hidden callbacks.
Victory/failure terminal state is explicit, versioned, hash-covered, save/load round-tripped, and explained through read models.
AI traces remain bounded, serializable, and tied to visible read-model inputs.
Major M6 UI surfaces cannot display placeholder-only panels for systems required by the contract.
Manual node battle, telemetry, accounts, paid services, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, commercial release, branding decisions, and irreversible migrations remain absent.

minimum_alpha_behavior: Operate in deterministic layers: survival/recovery, succession/legitimacy, diplomacy/obligation, policy/event, campaign/logistics, postwar/governance, and no-action/wait.
Submit the same command kinds available to the player, or choose no-action/wait with reason codes when no command is justified.
Create or update diplomacy offers, obligations, campaign objectives, succession support, policy choices, and postwar preferences only from visible state and bounded candidate lists.
Run a full Alpha scenario without lead rescue commands, hidden resource grants, hidden map knowledge, remote services, telemetry, or privileged mutation.
Explain target selection, refusal, wait, withdrawal, succession support, policy choice, and postwar choice through primary reason, rejected alternatives, and trace truncation metadata.
counterplay: Players can read why AI waited, refused, shifted target, withdrew, or supported a claimant and can respond through diplomacy, obligations, campaign timing, policy choices, or postwar terms.

AI can punish exposed costs such as over-mobilized labor, weak legitimacy, stale route knowledge, unpaid obligations, poor succession planning, or supply exhaustion, but only through normal commands and visible reasons.
constraints: No general hidden grand-strategy manager, no AI-only command surface, no runtime script engine, no server service, no telemetry, no arbitrary-code mod hook, and no manual node battle assumption.

No culture, ethnicity, or modern identity fixed preferences. AI preference comes from explicit scenario data, institutions, obligations, relationships, claims, logistics, knowledge age, risk, and policy/event state.

required_non_placeholder_surfaces: Population, labor, harvest, grain, cash, and obligation ledgers
Route, monsoon, supply, march, engagement, siege, withdrawal, and postwar outcome
Policy and event choices with causes and consequences
Encyclopedia references linked from scenario, event, institution, route, and culture-sensitive reviewed entries without claiming final content lock
Save/load checkpoint, seed, command count, state hash, and replay evidence
Local batch auto-run and balance dashboard artifacts
explanation_rules: Each major action preview shows top reason codes, concrete source-ledger deltas, institutional consequences, and known uncertainty.
The UI must distinguish actual control, suzerain relation, recognized legitimacy, obligations, claims, and postwar terms.
Manual node battle is not presented as an Alpha feature. If mentioned, it is only a post-1.0 non-blocking candidate note outside current implementation authorization.
Client code consumes protocol/query/read-model projections and does not duplicate sim-core formulas or hold full WorldState.
Text, controls, and status explanations must not overlap in supported Alpha desktop viewports; keyboard and focus paths cover the core flow.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: At least one scenario reaches the accepted victory condition or a defined failure state through authoritative sim, save, replay, and query paths.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: Terminal state has explicit schema, reason codes, stabilization evidence, and hash coverage.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Scripted or manual command log, save/load checkpoint, Node/Worker hash parity, and no invariant failures.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: Diplomacy and legitimacy can alter obligations, succession, campaign decisions, postwar outcomes, and AI reasons.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: Actors can accept, refuse, delay, contest, or recognize with serializable reasons; legitimacy has explicit sources.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Command parity tests, save/load tests, graph/reason invariants, and UI preview/read-model evidence.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: Succession and continuity can be resolved or fail inside Alpha without data corruption.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: Eligibility, claim strength, support sources, disputed outcome, and effects on obligations/recognition are visible.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Deterministic candidate tests, malformed input rejection, save round-trip, and start-to-victory integration.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: At least one policy/event choice affects the victory path or a failure/recovery path and links to encyclopedia context.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: Events have schema-defined cause, option, consequence, timing, and reference ids; no arbitrary scripts.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Content schema validation, deterministic event ordering tests, UI choice surface, and save/replay round-trip.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: Three Alpha scenarios compile with stable ids, source/claim labels, and references required by the major systems.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: Scenario data rejects unlabeled formal claims, bad references, duplicate ids, and unstable manifest output.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Content validation, manifest determinism, label audit, and historical_researcher routing for formal historical content.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: Map candidate data can be validated, compiled, and rendered as read-model-compatible geography for Alpha scenarios.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: District, route, water/land, ordering, and references are stable and schema-checked, but not final M7 map lock.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Map/content validation, renderer read-model tests, and no simulation authority leak from renderer.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: AI completes full Alpha runs without rescue and with bounded traces for strategic, diplomatic, succession, policy, campaign, and recovery decisions.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: AI acts through normal commands or reason-coded no-action/wait; no hidden resources or hidden manager.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: AI full-run tests, trace truncation, command parity, and batch no-rescue evidence.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: Player can start, understand, command, save/load, and finish or fail an Alpha run with all major systems visible.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: No required major system is represented only by placeholder text or inert mock panels.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Storybook or Playwright coverage, Web build, command preview/confirm smoke, and no client WorldState ownership.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: Local deterministic batch runs summarize victory, failure, AI collapse, economy pressure, succession, diplomacy, war/postwar losses, and balance risks.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: Dashboard reads local generated artifacts only; it is not telemetry or authoritative state.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Multiple scenario/seed smoke, artifact schema validation, dashboard rendering, and no remote collection.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: Core Alpha flows can be completed with keyboard reachability, focus visibility, labels, and non-color-only state.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: Accessibility evidence covers actual start, command, map, diplomacy, succession, policy/event, encyclopedia, save/load, and review flows.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Keyboard smoke, focus order, viewport overflow, non-color status, and screen-reader label spot checks.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: Adviser interventions are evaluated for whether they help players understand goals, risks, reasons, and victory/failure without taking control.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: Evidence distinguishes useful, ignored, harmful, and missing interventions with local/privacy-safe observation or deterministic run review.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Anonymized/local evidence, blocker/follow-up split, and no telemetry/network collection.

major_system_expectations.minimum_alpha_expectation: No accepted P0/P1 blocker remains for determinism, data corruption, save/load, command parity, security, package boundary, or start-to-victory playability.
major_system_expectations.not_placeholder_definition: Failures are recorded as BLOCKED or REQUEST_CHANGES, not downgraded to pass.
major_system_expectations.evidence_required: Full gate command set, focused M6 evidence, batch runs, accessibility evidence, and explicit known-limit review.

m1_m5_mapping.accepted_systems_used: Deterministic simulation authority, scheduler, command/event/query, RNG, content compiler baseline, save envelope, fixtures and hash evidence.
m1_m5_mapping.contract_use: All Alpha state changes and terminal proofs rely on deterministic command/replay/save/hash paths.

m1_m5_mapping.accepted_systems_used: District/world fixture, economy/population/labor scaffold, routes/transport, pressure validation, map renderer surface, save runtime.
m1_m5_mapping.contract_use: Population, labor, grain, cash, routes, transport capacity and season are concrete costs and constraints, not global penalties.

m1_m5_mapping.accepted_systems_used: Polity/vassalage, offices, characters, appointments, obligations, administrative burden, succession state machine, postwar governance, M3 save runtime.
m1_m5_mapping.contract_use: Victory requires institutions: offices, agents, obligations, succession continuity, administrative burden, and postwar or vassal settlement.

m1_m5_mapping.accepted_systems_used: Campaign objectives, knowledge, muster, grain supply, route capacity, march/supply state, automatic engagement, siege, withdrawal, postwar handoff, campaign AI, planning UI, determinism and stress evidence.
m1_m5_mapping.contract_use: War is a logistical and political sequence with automatic engagement only; campaign consequences feed back into postwar and institutions.

m1_m5_mapping.accepted_systems_used: Playable slice contract, sim loop, client playable UI, multi-year AI run, performance gate, Web/Windows smoke, player research, manual node battle deferral, M5 Gate PASS_WITH_LIMITS.
m1_m5_mapping.contract_use: M6 expands from vertical slice to Alpha system-complete proof while carrying forward known limits and preserving manual node battle deferral.

m6_dag_mapping.contract_role: Defines this reviewable Alpha system-complete contract.
Provides three validated Alpha scenarios and formal source/claim labels.
Provides a complete candidate map data-to-render path for Alpha scenarios.
Adds deterministic diplomacy and legitimacy state that affects victory, failure, AI, succession, and postwar terms.
Completes succession resolution required for continuity and recognized order.
Adds bounded policy/event and encyclopedia runtime participation.
Proves one complete authoritative start-to-victory or failure loop.
Proves no-rescue AI decision layers and reason traces across Alpha runs.
Exposes non-placeholder major system UI through read models and commands.
Validates core Alpha flow accessibility without claiming final release completion.
Provides local deterministic batch evidence and balance dashboard artifacts.
Records whether adviser interventions help or harm Alpha comprehension.
Validates no accepted P0/P1 blockers remain before exit consideration.
Synthesizes reviewed M6 evidence without starting M7 or reversing Human Gates.

evidence_requirements: Scenario id, scenario label mix, content/fixture manifest hash, start day, deterministic seed, start state hash, and host/platform are recorded.
Ordered command log includes actor kind, command kind, preview or submit result, rejection reasons, event tail identity, and terminal state transition.
At least one save/load checkpoint occurs before irreversible campaign or settlement commitment and one after settlement or failure.
Node runner and Worker/browser-compatible execution produce matching final state hash for the same seed and command sequence.

Victory evidence includes recognized arrangement, legitimacy/diplomacy source, succession continuity if relevant, obligations/governance terms, stabilization window, and no unresolved invariant breach.
Failure evidence identifies the explicit institutional, logistical, succession, diplomacy, policy/event, or invariant cause.
AI evidence includes selected candidate, primary reason, rejected alternative categories, no-action/wait/withdraw counts, trace truncation, and no hidden privilege.
UI evidence shows every major system surface is non-placeholder, command-driven, read-model-backed, and free of supported-viewport overlap in core flows.
Batch evidence is local, schema-versioned, deterministic, small enough for review, and not telemetry.
Adviser evidence is local/privacy-safe and classifies useful, ignored, harmful, and missing interventions.

Human Gate preservation evidence confirms manual node battle deferral, no telemetry, no accounts/secrets, no server/multiplayer, no arbitrary-code mods, no frozen product/platform/core change, no commercial/branding decision, no irreversible migration, and no major cultural-risk decision.

parameters.name: alpha_scenario_count
parameters.metric: All three compile and at least one reaches accepted victory while the others exercise pressure or failure/recovery evidence.

parameters.name: complete_playthrough_minutes
parameters.starting_hypothesis: 120-300 minutes for a first Alpha start-to-victory pass; shorter scripted evidence is acceptable for CI-like validation.
parameters.metric: Observed or scripted time to victory/failure, number of forced restarts, and number of unresolved blocker defects.

parameters.name: in_game_duration
parameters.starting_hypothesis: Two to six in-game years, enough for at least one campaign season, monsoon transition, succession or legitimacy pressure, policy/event consequence, and stabilization window.
parameters.metric: Run includes one season-driven planning change, one institutional consequence, and one terminal victory/failure explanation.

parameters.name: meaningful_decision_count
parameters.starting_hypothesis: Eight to fourteen meaningful player decisions on a successful path.
parameters.metric: Each counted decision has visible advantage, cost, dependency, counterplay, and reason-code evidence.
parameters.validation_way: Command log review, client flow smoke, and adviser intervention evidence.

parameters.name: stabilization_window_months
parameters.starting_hypothesis: Six to twelve in-game months after recognized settlement.
parameters.metric: No immediate invalidation from arrears, succession discontinuity, duplicate settlement, illegitimate recognition, source-ledger violation, or administrative collapse.
parameters.validation_way: Start-to-victory sim tests and Alpha no-P0/P1 validation.

parameters.name: ai_no_rescue_run_count
parameters.starting_hypothesis: At least one full no-rescue AI run per Alpha scenario plus a small deterministic seed set for batch smoke.
parameters.metric: No invariant failure, bounded trace size, bounded rejection rate, and terminal or continued-play state with explainable reasons.

parameters.name: major_surface_placeholder_tolerance
parameters.starting_hypothesis: Zero placeholder-only panels for contract-required major systems.
parameters.metric: Every required major surface has read-model data, reason text, available/rejected commands where relevant, and at least one covered state.

parameters.name: accessibility_core_flow_coverage
parameters.starting_hypothesis: Scenario start, command preview/confirm, map navigation, diplomacy, succession, policy/event, encyclopedia, save/load, and terminal review are keyboard reachable and labelable.
parameters.metric: Keyboard smoke, focus order, non-color state, label spot check, and viewport overflow results.

boundaries: Planning-only contract; no product code, package code, content-source data, dependency, release artifact, roadmap edit, accepted ADR edit, prior Gate edit, acceptance matrix edit, or implementation file is changed by this task.
M6 is Alpha system-complete proof, not M7 content lock and not M8 release hardening.
Manual node battle is deferred from M5 and the 1.0 mainline. It is not M6/M7/M8 implementation, spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency.

No full diplomacy, full peace treaty system, complex naval warfare, 3D battle, tactical battle UI, server, multiplayer, account, paid service, secret, telemetry, analytics pipeline, remote collection, arbitrary-code mod, runtime SQL database, ORM, Rust, or WASM is authorized.
No frozen product, platform, or core architecture change is authorized.
No commercial, release, store, branding, logo, trademark, signing, installer, public distribution, MANIFEST, SHA256SUMS, or VALIDATION_REPORT work is authorized.
No major cultural-risk decision or formal historical content lock is authorized; route formal content to historical_researcher before production acceptance.
No irreversible save/content migration is authorized.
human_gates: Frozen product, platform, or core architecture change
Arbitrary-code mods or runtime scripting as user extension
Commercial, release, store, branding, logo, or trademark decision
Manual node battle reversal or any formal 1.0 scope reversal

exploits.risk: Diplomacy offer spam scouts hidden acceptance thresholds.
exploits.counter: Preview can show known reasons, but submitted offers consume time, opportunity, relationship, obligation, or legitimacy hooks through explicit ledgers and cooldown-like institutional terms.

exploits.risk: Legitimacy becomes a single number players optimize while ignoring institutions.
exploits.counter: Legitimacy effects must cite specific claims, recognizers, offices, succession paths, obligations, events, and postwar terms.

exploits.risk: Succession support creates guaranteed puppet rulers.
exploits.counter: Candidates need eligibility, support sources, relationship context, local power, and refusal/contest paths; backing one claimant carries future costs.

exploits.risk: Direct control dominates every postwar choice.
exploits.counter: Direct control carries administrative, garrison, legitimacy, obligation, and local compliance costs that can be exploited by rivals or events.

exploits.risk: Vassal obligations create infinite troops, cash, grain, or access.
exploits.counter: Obligations and muster use source-ledger conservation, due dates, arrears, breach, partial fulfillment, and refusal reasons.

exploits.risk: Monsoon becomes a punitive global timer.
exploits.counter: Monsoon effects must be route-, river-, road-, supply-, siege-, disease-risk-, or timing-specific and show alternatives.

exploits.risk: Policy/events become arbitrary scripts that bypass command parity.
exploits.counter: Policy/events use explicit schemas, scheduler or command paths, deterministic ordering, and serializable effects only.

exploits.risk: AI wins or survives through hidden resources or hidden knowledge.
exploits.counter: AI traces cite visible faction knowledge, diplomacy, succession, policy/event, obligation, route, supply, and risk inputs; all actions use normal commands.
Batch artifacts are local generated files only, schema-versioned, reviewable, and never remotely collected by this contract.

exploits.risk: Encyclopedia or scenario text locks unreviewed history or cultural symbols.
exploits.counter: Formal historical and cultural details must carry source/claim labels and route through historical_researcher before production acceptance.

exploits.risk: Player treats actual-control map color as victory.
exploits.counter: Accepted victory requires recognized arrangement, legitimacy/diplomacy source, obligations/governance terms, and stabilization evidence.

vertical_slice_acceptance: This contract reaches REVIEW with gameplay_designer as writer and systems_architect as reviewer; gameplay_designer does not approve its own design implementation.
M6 downstream implementation tasks use this contract to prove one accepted start-to-victory Alpha path and explicit failure paths through authoritative command/query/save/replay surfaces.
All major M6 systems have non-placeholder sim and UI participation or reason-coded visible state before M6 exit can pass.
At least one full Alpha run records start hash, command sequence, save/load checkpoint, terminal outcome, final hash, Node/Worker-compatible parity, and no invariant failure.
AI can run complete Alpha scenarios without rescue and with bounded, visible reason traces.
Batch auto-run and balance dashboard evidence remain local, deterministic, and non-telemetry.
Accessibility core flows are covered for the Alpha start-to-terminal path without claiming final release accessibility completion.
Adviser intervention evidence records whether interventions improve player understanding without collecting telemetry or taking control.
M6 Alpha no-P0/P1 validation reports no accepted blocker for data corruption, determinism, save/load, command parity, security, package boundary, or start-to-victory playability.
M6 exit validation preserves M5 PASS_WITH_LIMITS context, DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE, Human Gates, and the boundary between M6 Alpha, M7 content lock, M8 release work, and post-1.0 candidates.

acceptance_evidence.advantage_cost_dependency_counterplay_ch: Every player_decisions entry includes advantage, cost, dependencies, and counter fields.
acceptance_evidence.manual_node_battle_allowed_mention: post-1.0 non-blocking candidate note only; not M6/M7/M8 implementation, spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency

notes: This is the first executable M6 task after the graph closes; it is planning-only and does not implement Alpha features.
The contract keeps M6 system-complete scope minimal and does not expand into M7 content lock, M8 release work, or post-1.0 expansion candidates.
If the contract needs a frozen decision change or manual node battle reversal, stop and route to Human Gate instead of continuing.
Contract is routed to systems_architect for review; gameplay_designer does not approve its own design implementation.
